job_snippets: Define product vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned to company OKRs; prioritize features using frameworks like RICE or MoSCoW.
Write detailed product requirements documents (PRDs), user stories, and acceptance criteria for engineering teams.
Conduct user research: interviews, surveys, usability tests; synthesize insights into product decisions.
Analyze product metrics using SQL and tools like Amplitude, Mixpanel, or Tableau; set up KPI dashboards.
Facilitate cross-functional collaboration between engineering, design, marketing, and sales teams.
Run agile ceremonies: sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and backlog grooming.
Launch products end-to-end: beta programs, go-to-market planning, release notes, and post-launch reviews.
Manage the product backlog in Jira or Linear; groom tickets, define milestones, and track velocity.
Conduct competitive analysis and market research; identify customer pain points and opportunities.
Define and monitor A/B experiments to validate hypotheses and measure feature impact on North Star metrics.
Build business cases and ROI models to justify product investments to leadership.
Partner with UX/design on wireframes, prototypes, and design reviews; champion user-centric decisions.
Communicate product strategy and progress through roadmap presentations to executives and stakeholders.
Work with data science teams to leverage ML/AI features for personalization, recommendations, or automation.
Strong understanding of technical architecture to assess feasibility, trade-offs, and technical debt.
Manage API products or developer platforms: documentation, versioning, partner integrations.
Lead discovery phases: assumption mapping, problem framing, Jobs-to-be-Done analysis.
Experience in B2B SaaS, consumer apps, or platform products with complex stakeholder environments.
Define pricing models, packaging tiers, and monetization strategies in collaboration with finance.
Build and mentor a team of associate PMs; establish PM culture and best practices.
